channel.title: +MT on आगमे शैवे विश्वासटिप्पन्यः
channel.description: Recent content in +MT on आगमे शैवे विश्वासटिप्पन्यः

item.title: 18 nAthas
item.description:
Source: TW

The pashchimAMnAya texts like the root samvartA-maNDala-sUtra-s, manthAnabhairava-tantra, and saMvarta-prakAsha state that first lineage of teaching from shiva and shakti (the haMsa dyad of this meditation) was transmitted by the 18 nAtha-s known as the khechara-s. The teachings of these 18 is referred to as the “aShTAdasha-divyAmnAyAnukramam.”

This 18 fold transmission is seen as the original divine layer atop the embodied transmissions of human nAtha-s. The latter consists of one branch of 3 lineages known as the oli-s with a teacher each in oDDiyAna, kAmarUpa and pUrNagiri and another branch of 9 nAtha-s.

item.title: KAmarUpa affiliation
item.description:
Source: TW

kAmarUpa, while today primarily worshipped under a traipura (dakShiNAmnAya) or a kAlIkula (a stream of uttarAmnAya) yAga,
appears to have originally been affiliated with the pashchimAmnAya.
In the kAlikApurANa, the primary kAmarUpa oriented text,
shiva teaches his sons, the vetAla and the bhairava (to be understood as baTuka) thus:

gachChataM kAmarUpAntaH
pIThaM nIlAchala+ahvayam ||
kAmAkhyA-nilayaM guhyaM
kubjikApITha-saMjnakam | AkAsha-gaNgA yatrAsti
taj-jalair abhiShichya cha || tatra+ArAdhayataM putrau
mahAmAyAM jaganmayIm |
sA prasannAchirAd devI
varadA nau bhaviShyati ||

item.title: pITha nAtha emanation
item.description:
The emanation of the 4 pITha-nAtha-s in the maNDala of pashchimAmnAya (the primordial siddhaugha), their nature and meditation on kubjikA conjoined with mitreshAna:

ओ-जा-पू-का
यस् त्व् अटेच् चैव त्रैलोक्ये
चिन्ताधारो महाप्रभुः ।
कामरूपांश-धारी च
चञ्चलानन्द उच्यते ॥

The one who roams about in the triple-world, bearing the thoughts, the great lord,
bearing kAmarUpa as his integral part, he is known as cha~nchalAnanda.

अणुरुपे स्थितो यस्मात्
पर्यटेत् सर्व-जन्तुषु । जालपीठे स्वाभावस् तु
आणवानन्द उच्यते ॥

item.title: SaMvartA-maNDala
item.description:
Source: TW

The pashchimAmnAya originally had a rather simple conception of the saMvartAmaNDala in the ShaT-sAhasra-saMhitA:

त्रिकोणं च चतुष्कोणं
षट्कोणं चैव बाह्यतः ।
तत्रोत्पन्नं जगत् सर्वं
तत्र लीनं चराचरम् ॥
वडवानल-मध्यस्थं
संवर्तामण्डलं तु तम् ।

There is a triangle, a square and a hexagon (hexastar) going outwards. There originates the entire world, and therein is retracted the animate and inanimate. Standing in the midst of the equine fire, this is indeed the saMvartAmaNDala.

item.title: Vajra gahvara prastara H
item.description:
Source: TW

A key maNDala of the pashchimAmnAya is the vajra-gahvara-prastAra. The 11 levels are believed to represent the 11 rudra-s. The 7 columns are the 7 mAtR^ikA-s. In the intersection of the atharvan tradition with the pashchimAmnaya, the prastAra may be deployed for apotropaic functions using the below atharvan incantation.

The deity of the prastAra is vajrakubjIshvarI or vajriNI a “kaulized” indrAnI.

Before drawing it, he should have a human yoginI of his kula lead the initial pUja. Then he installs it with his own kulA~NganA with the guidance of the kulAcharya. This is the simplest of the vajra-prastAra – the gahvara.

item.title: Vedic roots
item.description:
Source: TW

The samAvedic brAhmaNa-s have a cryptic allusion to the deva-s having supported the sun in the sky with sarasvatI. However, it collapsed, and hence it is supposed to be full of curves (tasmAt sA kubjimatIva) – a river with many bends. This was an attempt at drawing a sambandha between the heavenly sarasvatI – the Milky Way – and an earthly river.

An “out there” proposal:
Origin of the name kubjikA for the pashchimAmnAya goddess has been a bit of a mystery. She is only rarely shown with the stooping (kubja) iconography. Instead, we hold that it relates to her curve form as the kuNDalinI in the mUlAdhara.+++(4)+++
